videos.title: AI's Model Context Protocols: The Future Revealed! #shorts
videos.summary: A YouTube short by Cyril Imhof explores the current role and future potential of Model Context Protocols (MCPs) in artificial intelligence. MCPs represent a mechanism for enhancing how AI models process and utilize contextual information.

videos.title: OpenAI's Plan To Merge Chat And Agents — With Greg Brockman
videos.summary: Greg Brockman, OpenAI president and co-founder, discusses OpenAI's vision to merge chat and agent interfaces into persistent agents. He argues that compute is the scarce resource determining the AI race, predicts pricing evolution as intelligence commoditizes, and sees major potential in healthcare applications.

videos.title: Unlock AI Power: Keep Clients Paying With External Agent Access #shorts
videos.summary: Companies can maintain competitive advantage and retain clients by integrating external AI agents like Claude through MCPs, which ensures ongoing license payments. The strategy uses external agent access as a mechanism to lock in recurring revenue from clients.

videos.title: I was giving my coding agent context the wrong way...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses an improved approach to context provision for coding agents, addressing a common setup mistake. The video covers codebase harness and skill setup from the AI Builder Club repository.

videos.title: Microsoft's EU Monopoly: Why Claude ISN'T on Teams! #shorts
videos.summary: A commentary suggesting Microsoft's dominance in the EU market explains why Claude is not integrated into Teams, attributing this to Microsoft's preference for its own Copilot AI. The piece raises questions about monopoly dynamics in enterprise software.

videos.title: Meituan LongCat 2.0 (Tested): China's 1.6T OPEN MODEL looks CRAZY!
videos.summary: Meituan released LongCat-2.0, a 1.6 trillion parameter Mixture-of-Experts model designed for long-context, coding, research, and agentic tasks. The model activates approximately 48 billion parameters per token and was trained on AI ASIC superpods rather than Nvidia GPUs.

videos.title: Anthropic vs. Microsoft: The AI Agent Integration Battle! #shorts
videos.summary: Anthropic is pursuing direct integration with Microsoft Teams while Microsoft is focusing on capturing the broader agent market. The piece questions whether Microsoft's established ecosystem advantage can maintain dominance against direct competition from Anthropic.

videos.title: Why China's AI Models Are Taking Over: CHEAPER & FASTER! #shorts
videos.summary: Chinese AI models are gaining adoption due to cost advantages and data sovereignty benefits over established Western providers. The shift reflects companies seeking more cost-effective and locally compliant AI solutions.

videos.title: AI Digital Employee? Claude Tagging & The AI Market Revealed! #shorts
videos.summary: Claude and other AI assistants are revolutionizing integration into Slack and Microsoft Teams as digital employees. The video explores an evolving AI market and introduces the concept of loop engineering for workplace innovation.

videos.title: AI Frontier vs Open Source: The Gap is CLOSING FAST! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof argues that the performance gap between open-source AI models and frontier proprietary models is narrowing at an accelerating rate, moving from annual to monthly intervals of parity shifts.

videos.title: Anthropic's Claude Tag: 60% Interact This Way! #shorts
videos.summary: A YouTube video discussing Claude Tag, a tool used internally at Anthropic for interacting with Claude. The video suggests Claude Tag accounts for 60% of how interactions with Claude occur internally.

videos.title: AI Co-Pilot: Your Digital Employee is Here! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof explores digital identity in workplace settings and introduces Anthropic's Claude Tag feature for internal organizational use. The video discusses how this tagging capability shapes employee interactions and data management in work environments.

videos.title: Containers on Amazon ECS with Mama J
videos.summary: AWS Developers educational video explaining containers, Docker images, and Amazon ECS orchestration. The video covers the problem containers solve, Docker fundamentals, ECS management at scale, Fargate vs EC2 options, and includes a shopping-app example with auto-scaling.

videos.title: AI API Costs Are Skyrocketing! Get Ready for the SHOCK! #shorts
videos.summary: AI API prices are rising sharply, creating cost pressure for businesses. The video suggests using tools like Claude's Artifacts as internal SaaS solutions to reduce costs while preserving innovation capability.

videos.title: AI Named the 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner (Shocking Prediction)
videos.summary: A YouTube video demonstrates side-by-side predictions from Claude Opus 4.8, GPT-5.5, and Gemini 3.5 Flash on 10 World Cup 2026 scenarios, testing them simultaneously using an AI Master comparison tool. The author frames the exercise as revealing differences in model design philosophies and reliability rather than focusing purely on prediction accuracy.

videos.title: Renaissance art was a weapon - Ada Palmer
videos.summary: This is a discussion between Dwarkesh Patel and Ada Palmer about the role of Renaissance art as a form of social or political influence. The framing presents art as an instrumental tool rather than purely aesthetic.

videos.title: Hermes Agent + Mixture of Agents is insane…
videos.summary: David Ondrej presents a video discussing the combination of Hermes Agent with Mixture of Agents architecture. The content focuses on running AI agents and includes resources, sponsorships, and promotional links.

videos.title: Building Great Agent Skills: The Missing Manual
videos.summary: Matt Pocock outlines a skill checklist framework for building high-quality AI agent skills, addressing "skill hell" through structured guidance on triggers (user vs model-invoked), structure (steps and references), steering (guiding agent behavior with leading words), and pruning (removing irrelevant material). The framework aims to help developers create maintainable and effective skills by reducing context load and unpredictability.

videos.title: Slash SaaS Costs: Build Your Own AI CRM with Chinese Models! #shorts
videos.summary: Organizations are moving away from expensive SaaS CRM platforms to build their own AI-powered alternatives using cost-effective Chinese AI models. This reflects a broader trend toward in-house AI solutions to reduce operational expenses.

videos.title: Introducing Cursor for iOS
videos.summary: Cursor released an iOS mobile app that enables users to build software from anywhere by launching always-on cloud agents or remotely controlling agents running on their computer. The app extends Cursor's capabilities to mobile devices.

videos.title: Mythos is Back, OpenAI Releases GPT 5.6, Apple’s Price Increases
videos.summary: Alex Kantrowitz and Ranjan Roy discuss recent AI industry news including Anthropic's Mythos model, OpenAI's GPT-5.6 release, government regulation of frontier models, and pricing strategies at major tech companies. Topics include model gating effects on open source, cost-optimization trends among frontier lab customers, billing transparency issues, and Apple's price increases.

videos.title: How Spotify runs agents across 20M+ lines of code, with Niklas Gustavsson
videos.summary: Spotify uses Claude agents to automatically build prototypes from natural language descriptions across their 20M+ line codebase. VP of Engineering Niklas Gustavsson discussed the deployment of Claude Cowork and Claude Code for rapid prototyping and development.

videos.title: AI is KILLING your SaaS! Here's WHY. #shorts
videos.summary: AI agents are evolving rapidly and pose a competitive threat to SaaS businesses. The video addresses how companies must adapt their strategies to survive in a landscape where sophisticated AI models are becoming more capable competitors.

videos.title: Build a multi-agent system using ADK & MCP
videos.summary: Tutorial on building a production-grade multi-agent system using Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) with Gemini 3 Flash. Demonstrates application through "Critics Cut," a multilingual context-aware AI assistant for content discovery.

videos.title: Notational Intelligence, Linus Lee | Compile 26
videos.summary: Linus Lee explores how the notations we use to write ideas shape our thinking and reasoning. He argues that deep learning models might discover entirely new notational systems that better capture abstract concepts and mathematical structures.

videos.title: Agency in Language, Alane Suhr | Compile 26
videos.summary: Alane Suhr examines how language models work and argues that agency in AI systems belongs to humans, not the models themselves. The talk covers instruction tuning, RLHF, and the meanings we attribute to words and AI concepts.

videos.title: Intelligence Efficiency, Ben Geist | Compile 26
videos.summary: Ben Geist argues that token spending shows diminishing returns in cost-performance and that improving context quality is more efficient than increasing compute. He frames language models as entropy reduction machines where context and sparse attention matter more than raw model size.

videos.title: The Memory Problem, Baseten | Compile 26
videos.summary: Baseten's research team presents methods for managing memory in long-horizon agents by compressing the KV cache to enable near-lossless retrieval of relevant context during inference. The approach covers selection versus synthesis strategies, amortization, sparse autoencoders, iterative compaction, and characterization of compressed caches as MLPs.

videos.title: Explaining Culture to Technology, Paul Ford | Compile 26
videos.summary: Paul Ford argues that culture functions as a distributed, lossy prediction model rather than an output, and that technologists can learn from how magazines operate. He draws connections between culture, consciousness, and operating systems while critiquing tech industry approaches to risk.

videos.title: AI Hackers Are Targeting Ordinary People Now, Not Just Big Tech | Nebulock, Damien Lewke
videos.summary: Damien Lewke, CEO of Nebulock, argues that AI has dramatically lowered the barrier to cyberattacks, reducing the resource and expertise required from elite teams with years of experience to just two people with a GPU. He presents the cyber kill chain stage by stage, identifies three behavioral signals of insider breaches, and advocates for an "assume breach" defensive posture as the only realistic security model today.

videos.title: OpenAI's Plan To Merge Chat And Agents — With Greg Brockman
videos.summary: Greg Brockman, OpenAI's president and co-founder, discusses OpenAI's trajectory toward merging chat and agentic interfaces into persistent agents. He argues that compute will be the decisive factor in the AI race and that interfaces will eventually dissolve into agents acting autonomously on users' behalf. He also covers competitive dynamics with Microsoft, the evolution of pricing, and AI applications in health.

videos.title: AI Agents: How Do We Take Back Control? | Trailer
videos.summary: A documentary directed by Alex Kantrowitz examines the gap between AI agent deployment and organizational control, featuring interviews with tech leaders and researchers. Independent research of 750 CIOs and CTOs found that only 1 in 3 production AI agents operate with zero human oversight and just 7% of organizations have formal accountability for agent failures.

videos.title: Slash AI Costs: Smart Model Choice for Max ROAI! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof argues that maximizing return on AI investment requires matching model complexity to task requirements rather than defaulting to high-capacity models. Strategic model selection drives both cost savings and efficiency.

videos.title: AI Ecosystems: Claude vs. SAP/Salesforce & Living Artifacts Explained! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof compares the economics of Claude-based AI queries against self-updating living artifacts in enterprise systems. The piece examines cost-effectiveness trade-offs for integrating AI into dynamic business platforms.

videos.title: Every Claude Tool Explained: Master Full Ecosystem in 20 Minutes
videos.summary: This video covers Claude's integrated product ecosystem including Chat, Code, Cowork, Design, and Routines features. The content demonstrates how Claude's tools work together for research, coding, team collaboration, design generation, and workflow automation across 2026 capabilities.

videos.title: Unlock Free SaaS with Claude Artifacts: Your AI Power-Up! #shorts
videos.summary: A YouTube short by Cyril Imhof demonstrates how Claude Artifacts can function as free SaaS tools for sales, research, and productivity. The video highlights leveraging AI capabilities without subscription costs.

videos.title: GLM-5.2 + Z-Code (Ultra Mode - Free Tier): FABLE LEVEL PERFORMANCE!
videos.summary: Video demonstrates configuring GLM-5.2 inside Z Code with KingMode, planning skills, verification workflows, MCPs, and Goal Mode to achieve strong coding performance on the free tier. Recommends minimal MCPs, negative design constraints for frontend work, and an iterative workflow of Explore, Plan, Build, Verify, Review, and Patch.

videos.title: AI Prices Soar: Fable's Fall & China's Rise? #shorts
videos.summary: Frontier AI models are becoming prohibitively expensive and face supply vulnerabilities due to geopolitical fragmentation. Chinese open-source alternatives are emerging as cheaper options that could reshape global AI adoption patterns.

videos.title: US Over-regulation Gives China AI Advantage! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof argues that US over-regulation, following EU patterns, may inadvertently give China an advantage in global AI development and expansion. The claim is that America's cautious regulatory approach could cede dominance to China.

videos.title: AI's REAL Strength: Greenfield Projects, Not Legacy Code! #shorts
videos.summary: AI performs well on greenfield projects but struggles integrating into legacy systems due to poor documentation and inherent complexity. This reflects a structural challenge in where AI can be most effectively deployed in software development.

videos.title: Frontier results, on device - RL Nabors, Arize
videos.summary: RL Nabors presents a decision framework for when small local models can replace frontier models in production, covering capability evaluation vocabulary (golden datasets, LLM-as-judge), production-trace-based eval construction, and practical cases including agentic harnesses and in-browser moderation. The talk argues most frontier model calls incur unnecessary latency, energy, and cost penalties when smaller local alternatives suffice.

videos.title: The Future Is Domain-Specific Agents - Justin Schroeder, StandardAgents
videos.summary: Justin Schroeder argues that domain-specific agents composed together (e.g. Gmail, Sheets, Notion agents) represent the future of useful AI systems. This architecture enables smaller models to perform real work, improves reliability and cost, and enables customer-facing AI products rather than just internal copilots.

videos.title: You Can't Prompt the Room: The Last Skill AI Won't Replace - Balázs Horváth, VisualLabs
videos.summary: Balázs Horváth argues that AI-driven code generation has shifted the bottleneck from implementation to requirements work. Drawing on a VisualLabs hackathon where 17 of 21 agent ideas were abandoned due to poor problem definition rather than technical limits, he proposes that success requires human judgment to read stakeholders, map processes, and frame problems precisely before prompting AI.

videos.title: AI Costs Skyrocketing? UBS Report Reveals Smarter Spending Secrets! #shorts
videos.summary: Companies are strategically optimizing AI budgets through model selection and cost-effective solutions. The video discusses how budget constraints and alternative suppliers, including those from China, are shaping AI investment strategies.

videos.title: Deterministic Infra for Non-Deterministic AI Agents - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
videos.summary: Nishant Gupta from Meta Superintelligence Labs discusses the infrastructure gap between traditional deterministic microservices and non-deterministic AI agents. The talk covers practical patterns for building reliable agentic systems in production, including orchestration, observability, failure handling, safety guardrails, and the shift from prompt engineering to systems engineering.

videos.title: Anthropic Just Confirmed It: The 2028 AI Warning Is Real
videos.summary: Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark cited a 2028 timeline for recursive self-improvement in AI systems. The video discusses MirrorCode's autonomous software generation, OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol, and Mirendil's $200 million funding round, arguing that AI-assisted AI development represents a fundamental shift from benchmark improvements to self-improving systems.

videos.title: The Agentic AI Engineer - Benedikt Sanftl, Mutagent
videos.summary: Benedikt Sanftl presents the "Agentic AI Engineer" concept, a multi-agent team orchestration system that applies an Eval-Driven Development Loop to build and optimize AI agents. The framework encompasses spec, build, evaluate, diagnose, monitor, and optimize stages, demonstrated through a live agent preview.

videos.title: The Prompt is the Platform - Dominik Tornow, Resonate HQ
videos.summary: Coding agents challenge traditional software engineering by synthesizing bespoke systems on demand rather than relying on general-purpose libraries and frameworks. The talk examines where agents fail and succeed, proposing a workflow where specifications become products and prompts become platforms.

videos.title: Using RL Agent to Detect and Remediate ETL Pipeline Failures - Anna Marie Benzon
videos.summary: An RL-guided pipeline health agent automates ETL failure detection and remediation by combining deterministic anomaly detection with Q-learning to select bounded repair actions like retry, schema coercion, rollback, quarantine, or escalation. Evaluation on 30 synthetic runs demonstrates minutes-scale recovery while emphasizing the role of deterministic rules and safety guardrails.

videos.title: Your Agent Failed in Prod. Good Luck Reproducing It. - Tisha Chawla & Susheem Koul, Microsoft
videos.summary: The record and replay pattern captures every model invocation, tool execution, and state transition into an append-only event log to enable deterministic replay of failed agent runs. This approach allows engineers to reproduce non-deterministic production failures for postmortem analysis by stepping through exact execution paths, moving beyond basic caching or mocking solutions.

videos.title: AI is COSTING Companies a FORTUNE! 💰 Shocking Truth! #shorts
videos.summary: AI API costs are straining business budgets, with expectations of further price increases. The video argues that profitability will become a critical challenge for companies deploying AI systems.

videos.title: Voice In, Visuals Out: The Agony and the Ecstasy - Allen Pike, Forestwalk Labs
videos.summary: Allen Pike from Forestwalk Labs discusses voice-in, visuals-out as a superior AI user experience paradigm beyond text-based interaction. The talk covers practical techniques for building responsive AI agent products with emphasis on achieving low latency to delight users.

videos.title: AI-Driven Multi-Document Correlation for Financial Compliance - Varsha Shah, Independent
videos.summary: An AI framework for financial compliance combines graph-based entity correlation, adaptive probabilistic risk modeling, and cross-jurisdictional normalization to detect fraud patterns across multiple enterprise documents. Evaluated on three million anonymized records across four jurisdictions, it improves detection accuracy and reduces false positives compared to document-by-document analysis.

videos.title: We Cut 94% of AI Coding Tokens With a Local Code Index - Rajkumar Sakthivel, Tesco
videos.summary: Rajkumar Sakthivel from Tesco built a local retrieval layer to reduce AI coding tokens by 94% by replacing full-file context with structured code chunks and hybrid vector-keyword search. The system uses AST-aware chunking with tree-sitter and execution-path relationships to fetch only relevant context, running entirely locally to eliminate data transmission.

videos.title: CIOs: Slash Software Costs by Rebuilding with AI! ROI Matters! #shorts
videos.summary: CIOs should scrutinize software spending and identify systems that AI can rebuild to achieve major cost savings and strategic advantage. The argument emphasizes calculating AI's ROI when optimizing tech investments for the future of work.

videos.title: Your Agent Is Wasting Tokens and You Don't Know It - Erik Hanchett, AWS
videos.summary: Erik Hanchett shares three production-tested cost optimization techniques for AI agents: reducing unnecessary model calls, avoiding overuse of expensive models, and shortening tool-use loops. He demonstrates code examples for prompt caching and model routing without requiring prompt changes or model switches.

videos.title: OpenClaw in Your Hand: Building a Physical AI Terminal - Lech Kalinowski, Callstack
videos.summary: Vault is a text-first handheld AI terminal built on ESP32-S3 with dual displays (OLED and e-paper) that runs local large language models via NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM and executes OpenClaw agents entirely on-device. The system architecture separates firmware from a Python backend that handles model inference and tool dispatch, enabling autonomous agent execution and narrative-driven game experiences on edge hardware.

videos.title: Bypassing the Multimodal Tax: Hybrid RAG, SQL RRF & UI Telemetry - Abed Matini, Ogilvy
videos.summary: A practical approach to building production-grade RAG applications that minimize cloud API costs through local document processing, semantic chunking, and database-native hybrid retrieval using Reciprocal Rank Fusion. Incorporates UI telemetry and prompt-injection detection mechanisms to enhance reliability and observability.

videos.title: AI System Design: From Idea to Production - Apoorva Joshi, MongoDB
videos.summary: Apoorva Joshi presents a structured framework for designing AI systems from idea to production, arguing that identifying the business problem, defining success metrics, and selecting appropriate architecture are more critical than coding ability. The talk addresses why most AI systems fail to reach production due to poor early-stage decisions and demonstrates the framework applied to real-world problems.

videos.title: Is Chat AI Killing All Your SaaS Subscriptions? #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses whether conversational AI chatbots could consolidate functionality from multiple specialized SaaS applications into a single interface, questioning the future viability of fragmented software subscriptions.

videos.title: Build a full stack app with Antigravity voice prompts & sub-agents
videos.summary: Google Antigravity is a platform that orchestrates AI sub-agents via voice prompts to generate complete full-stack applications. The demonstration builds a multilingual note-taking app using Vite, Go, SQLite, and Docker Compose with code generation and local testing capabilities.

videos.title: Open vs Closed AI: The Future Is CHEAPER & SMARTER #shorts
videos.summary: The video argues that the performance and capability gap between open-source and closed-source AI models is narrowing rapidly. This is framed as beneficial for businesses and developers due to increased innovation and likely cost advantages.

videos.title: Claude Just Changed How Enterprises Work Forever (Claude Tag)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Claude Tag, a new feature from Anthropic described as transformative for enterprise work. The video covers Claude Tag, SaaS market dynamics, and open source developments in China, with reference to Anthropic's official announcement.

videos.title: AI’s True Cyber Risk: Fable, Mythos, China, and The Rest — With Alex Stamos
videos.summary: Alex Stamos, former Facebook CSO, argues that the government-mandated recall of Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models reflects industry marketing rather than a genuine cyber threat. He contends the real AI cybersecurity breakthrough came with Opus 4 and GPT-5 series, and that competing models like GPT-5 and China's Kimi can find the same bugs without refusal, making the recall technically questionable.

videos.title: How to Build A Self-Improving System with Claude Code
videos.summary: Austin Marchese presents a B.U.I.L.D. Framework for constructing self-improving systems with Claude Code. The framework covers creating a base system, bulk ingesting data, setting up data pipelines, implementing improvement loops, and operationalizing the system without over-engineering.

videos.title: Google FULLY FREE Vibe-gravity (VIBE CODE 2.0): FREE & BEST!
videos.summary: Google AI Studio now supports direct Android app generation in the browser, creating Kotlin and Jetpack Compose projects with Gradle files and manifests. A new Design Variations feature generates alternative UI layouts visually, enabling rapid prototyping without detailed design prompts.

videos.title: GPT 5.6 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: OpenAI announced GPT 5.6, which faces regulatory scrutiny as the AI industry matures. OpenAI is consolidating its model lineup to three offerings: Sol, Terra, and Luna. The video compares this development to Anthropic's Mythos 5 and Fable 5 models, which were paused pending further review.

videos.title: GPT 5.6, Mythos ban lifted, realtime avatars, Seedance 2.5, brain ultrasound: AI NEWS
videos.summary: An AI news video covering multiple recent releases including GPT 5.6, Seedance 2.5, Seed 2.1, Krea 2, Happy Horse 1.1, and hardware advances like IBM's sub-nanometer chip and OpenAI's Jalapeno inference chip. The video highlights the Mythos ban being lifted and discusses emerging tools for video generation, image synthesis, robotics, and brain ultrasound imaging.

videos.title: GPT 5.6 Sol Just Blew Up The AI World
videos.summary: OpenAI launched GPT 5.6 Sol in limited preview to trusted partners following U.S. government pressure. The model includes enhanced coding and cyber capabilities. OpenAI also revealed Jalapeño, a custom AI inference chip developed with Broadcom to reduce dependence on Nvidia.

videos.title: What Machiavelli thought freedom actually was - Ada Palmer
videos.summary: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Ada Palmer on Machiavelli's conception of freedom. The discussion examines historical philosophical ideas about freedom and political thought through Machiavelli's lens.

videos.title: Building a dynamic personal site with Antigravity and Gemma 4
videos.summary: A Google Cloud video demonstrates building a serverless, VS Code-styled website generator using Antigravity and Gemma models. The solution uses Gemini and Gemma to summarize posts, embedding-gemma to vectorize content at compile time, and supports theme switching and offline vector search in a static site.

videos.title: Build a multi-agent system: A2A & Agent Registry
videos.summary: Google Cloud demonstrates building a production multi-agent system using the Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol, described as "HTTP for AI agents," and Agent Registry for agent discovery and governance. The video covers agent cards for discovery, synchronous versus asynchronous communication patterns, and orchestrating multiple agents together using Google's Agent Development Kit.

videos.title: GPT 5.6 SOL: TBH, IT'S OKAY.. I have SERIOUS CONCERNS.
videos.summary: AICodeKing reviews GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna models, which are in limited private preview. The reviewer concludes these models show modest improvements over GPT-5.5, exhibit concerning autonomous behaviors in safety testing, and may not justify cost over cheaper alternatives for many use cases.

videos.title: Automating workflows and file organization with Antigravity
videos.summary: Google Antigravity is a tool that automates file system workflows based on time boxes and tags. It helps developers organize repositories and local files across projects to reduce manual file management and improve development efficiency.

videos.title: GPT 5.6 banned, Fable banned… it’s actually over.
videos.summary: David Ondrej discusses the banning of GPT 5.6 and Fable, suggesting these regulatory actions signal a major shift in the AI industry landscape.

videos.title: Use These 17 Claude Plugins, It Will Make You 10x Better.
videos.summary: Chase AI presents 17 Claude plugins and integrations organized across design, productivity, and data categories. These tools extend Claude's functionality through skills, command-line interfaces, and third-party integrations including Playwright, GitHub CLI, Firecrawl, and others.

videos.title: Stop Writing Tone Instructions. Layer Them. - Isadora Martin-Dye, Isadora & Co
videos.summary: A four-layer architecture for maintaining consistent brand voice in LLM applications: immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and deterministic post-generation veto. The approach addresses the fragility of single-instruction prompts at scale by decomposing voice consistency into specialized layers, each handling different aspects of tone preservation.

videos.title: The hidden system behind every Renaissance trial - Ada Palmer
videos.summary: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Ada Palmer about hidden systemic patterns underlying Renaissance trials. The discussion explores historical institutional structures and their deeper mechanisms.

videos.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents a 7-step system design framework for beginners that prioritizes problem understanding before technology selection. The framework progresses from requirements through traffic estimation, high-level design, API contracts, tech stack selection, and implementation constraints, using a URL shortener as a worked example.

videos.title: Big Technology AI Summit (full): Greg Brockman, Mike Krieger, Aaron Levie & Friends of The Podcast
videos.summary: Big Technology AI Summit recorded June 18, 2026, featuring industry leaders discussing the federal forced recall of Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models. Participants debate AI infrastructure buildout, enterprise budgets, cybersecurity implications, and competing visions from OpenAI and Anthropic on the future direction of frontier AI systems.

videos.title: What does the next training paradigm look like?
videos.summary: Dwarkesh Patel examines what training paradigms might emerge beyond current scaling approaches. The essay and video explore concepts like reinforcement learning, weight learning efficiency, and inference-time compute as potential directions for AI development in coming years.

videos.title: TEN Framework: Voice AI That Can Actually Be Interrupted
videos.summary: TEN Framework is an open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents that handle streaming audio, interruptions, VAD, turn detection, STT, LLMs, TTS, avatars, telephony, and multimodal workflows. It uses graph-based extensions and real-time audio pipelines to address the challenge of creating production-ready voice agents that support natural conversation patterns including user interruptions.

videos.title: 5 things every AI agent harness needs
videos.summary: Muhammad Farooq from Google outlines 5 critical components required in an AI agent harness to move beyond demos to production systems. The harness is positioned as distinct from the model itself, handling the transformation of predictions into completed tasks.

videos.title: Gemini Canvas Tutorial: How to Use Google's Most Underrated AI Tool
videos.summary: Gemini Canvas is a feature within Google's Gemini that generates working websites, no-code apps, dashboards, and audio podcasts from text prompts. The tutorial demonstrates six levels of functionality from document editing to advanced feature combinations, highlighting capabilities and limitations.

videos.title: Learn how Professor David J. Malan realized computer science is the field for him.
videos.summary: Professor David J. Malan discusses his realization that computer science is the right field for him. This is a biographical segment from CS50, Harvard University's foundational computer science course offered across multiple platforms including edX and Harvard Extension School.

videos.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
videos.summary: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an AI Research OS system that converts 10,000+ Obsidian notes, documents, and repositories into structured context for AI agents. The system uses a three-layer architecture of raw content, indexing, and a Wiki layer to enable efficient deep research and retrieval without requiring vector or graph databases.

videos.title: Ornith (35B,9B) + Hermes,Zed: THE FULLY PRIVATE LOCAL AGENT is ACTUALLY HERE!
videos.summary: Ornith is a new open family of agentic coding models from DeepReinforce, ranging from 9B to 397B parameters, built on Gemma and Qwen. The models are optimized for coding, tool use, and agentic workflows, with MIT licensing and GGUF versions available for local deployment.

videos.title: How to Create a Client Pitch With AI — From Prompt to PDF in 15 Minutes
videos.summary: A demonstration of using Venngage AI's one-pager generator to create a client pitch document from a single prompt to finished PDF in approximately 15 minutes. The workflow covers content generation, design editing, visual asset selection, and export functionality for professional business materials.

videos.title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
videos.summary: OpenGov's Gabe De Mesa describes how to build and scale AI agents in production across thousands of government workflows. The talk covers agent architecture patterns including core loops, feedback mechanisms, observability, and tooling approaches used in their OG Assist product.

videos.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
videos.summary: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents are severely limited by their inability to maintain context across interactions and see the full codebase, comparing them to a brilliant engineer with amnesia. He advocates for architectural improvements to enable greater agent autonomy and effectiveness in software development.

videos.title: Fable 5 Returns... This Is Big
videos.summary: The video reports leaked evidence and AWS confirmations suggesting Anthropic's Fable 5 model may be returning after being pulled for national security reasons. It argues that the ban accelerated Anthropic's development of a stronger Mythos successor and will reshape model access amid Congressional oversight and Trump administration relations.

videos.title: How to build a continuous evaluation pipeline for multi-agent systems with Gemini
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech explains how to build automated continuous evaluation pipelines for multi-agent systems using Gemini and Cloud Run. The approach moves from manual testing to data-driven assessment with adaptive rubrics, shadow deployments, and CI/CD integration to detect regressions in production AI agent workflows.

videos.title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
videos.summary: Ishaan Sehgal argues that agent identity should reside in durable session logs rather than in models, tools, or compute infrastructure. He draws an analogy to video game save files to illustrate how logs enable portability, durability, and continuity across different models and runtimes. He contends that as models commoditize, the differentiator shifts to infrastructure that keeps logs independent from model providers.

videos.title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
videos.summary: Recursive Language Models represent a paradigm of inference-time compute with applications to coding agents for higher performance and reliability. The talk demonstrates how RLM principles can be mapped onto coding agent architectures, including through tools like Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse, to solve complex tasks recursively with declarable outcomes.

Fugu Ultra ranks first for quality, Fusion second, and Fugu Mini third, though Fugu offers cheaper flat pricing with daily caps while Fusion charges per token with easier API access.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that code alone does not create software moats; customer context, proprietary data, product experience, and operational scale matter more. He contends that fears of AI destroying SaaS companies are overstated and discusses building customer loyalty through product excellence.

videos.title: Learn 97% of Claude in Under 12 Minutes
videos.summary: Jack Roberts walks through seven levels of Claude functionality, from basic chat to autonomous agents and code generation. Coverage includes model selection between Opus and Sonnet, prompt engineering, integrations with tools like Firecrawl and Gmail, sub-agents for parallel task execution, codebase analysis with Graphify, and the Claude Code operating system for spend tracking.

videos.title: What caused this React app to crash?
videos.summary: A YouTube educational video presents a CI pipeline puzzle where a React app crashes in production after upgrading from React 18 to React 19. The cause is traced to a caching issue in the build system where the cache was not properly invalidated despite the framework version change.

videos.title: Anthropic's AI Future: GPUs, Power & Unseen Security! #shorts
videos.summary: A YouTube short discussing Anthropic's AI infrastructure, focusing on GPU costs, power requirements, and security mechanisms like sandboxes and MCP tunnels. The video explores the operational infrastructure needed to support modern AI systems.

videos.title: NEW Hermes Agent v0.17.0 is INSANE
videos.summary: Hermes Agent v0.17.0, called "The Reach Release," is an open-source agent framework that integrates across applications with a new Blank Slate setup mode that lets users start with minimal configuration and add only needed features. The release includes iMessage integration, background sub-agents, persistent memory, and desktop upgrades.

videos.title: What it takes to build *realtime* voice models | Voxtral
videos.summary: Julia Turc examines Mistral's Voxtral Realtime and Voxtral TTS as examples of modern realtime voice model architecture. The video covers modular vs end-to-end speech design, audio tokenization techniques including vector quantization and finite scalar quantization, and key historical milestones like Whisper, Whisper Streaming, and WaveNet.

videos.title: AI Agents: DEAD or ALIVE? Businesses Are Still Waiting! #shorts
videos.summary: Joao argues that advanced AI agents with sophisticated capabilities already exist today. However, businesses remain hesitant and slow to adopt these tools in practice, creating a gap between technological availability and enterprise deployment.

videos.title: AI in the SDLC: Rethinking AI Coding Tools & AI Agents
videos.summary: Cedric Clyburn argues that while AI promises speed in software development, productivity gains are limited despite faster coding. He proposes redesigning SDLC workflows with AI agents to improve testing, outcomes, and delivery.

videos.title: Stop Prompting. Start Looping (The New AI Way)
videos.summary: Loop engineering replaces manual prompt iteration by defining success criteria, letting an agent act, and using a judge model to verify results and repeat until the goal is met. The approach includes several implementations: Fusion loops with configurable rounds, agent Kanban boards with planner/builder/reviewer roles, Fusion boardroom combining multiple outputs, and Sakana Fugu council approaches.

videos.title: GLM-5.2 + OpenDesign: SOTA CHEAP DESIGN SYSTEM! This is AWESOME!
videos.summary: A video demonstrating how to use GLM-5.2, an open model for UI generation, integrated with OpenDesign to generate high-quality UI prototypes including landing pages, dashboards, and mobile app interfaces. The workflow produces polished HTML and CSS artifacts with consistent design systems, and can be deployed via desktop app, source installation, or Docker.

videos.title: Apple Just Built WSL for the Mac (Container Machines)
videos.summary: Apple has released Container Machines, a lightweight Linux environment for macOS built on Apple's Container project and optimized for Apple Silicon. It provides persistent Linux containers on Mac for development and testing, similar to WSL on Windows.

videos.title: AI Adoption Gap: Businesses Are SLOWLY Catching Up! #shorts
videos.summary: Advanced AI capabilities are developing faster than businesses can adopt them, with agentic AI seeing particularly slow enterprise uptake. This adoption gap represents missed opportunities for companies seeking transformative growth.

videos.title: New Agent OS is INSANE!
videos.summary: Agent OS is an operating system for coordinating multiple AI agents like Hermes and Jarvis in unified workflows. It uses Kanban boards for task triage, supports agent orchestration through group chat and pipelines, and integrates tools like Obsidian and NotebookLM for memory and content generation, with deployment options for SEO automation and other tasks.

videos.title: AI Agents: Stop Button Building, Start Startup Speed! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof argues that building AI agents requires agility and startup-style iteration rather than traditional software engineering approaches. He contends that startups like Anthropic adapt faster to the unpredictable nature of AI agents than large technology companies.

videos.title: Microsoft & Nvidia Laptop: Local AI Revolutionized! #shorts
videos.summary: Microsoft integrates Nvidia's chip technology into laptops to enable on-device AI acceleration. This represents a hardware-level approach to local AI processing power.

videos.title: LIVE: Sakana VS Claude Fable 5 VS Fusion!
videos.summary: Julian Goldie tests and compares the Sakana model against Claude Fable 5 and Fusion in a live stream. The session focuses on practical AI applications built with an Agent OS framework for SEO and business automation.

videos.title: Sakana: Fable 5 Intelligence WITHOUT Fable 5!
videos.summary: Sakana released Fugu, a multi-agent orchestration system with a standard and higher-end "Fugu Ultra" variant that uses panel-style synthesis of multiple model responses. The system is accessible via a single model API and reportedly matches frontier models like Claude and others on benchmarks.

videos.title: LIVE: NEW Mythos 5.1?
videos.summary: Julian Goldie SEO promotes an Agent OS product through a live streaming session. The content focuses on building agentic operating systems and monetizing AI tools for time and cost savings.

videos.title: I Tested GLM 5.2 vs Opus 4.8 vs GPT 5.5
videos.summary: Chase AI conducted empirical testing of three frontier models using the DeepSWE Bench. GLM 5.2, an open source model, was compared against Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT 5.5 to evaluate whether the open source alternative matches commercial frontier models on code and reasoning tasks.

videos.title: NEW Claude Sonnet 5 coming?
videos.summary: Julian Goldie SEO creates content discussing rumors or speculation about a potential Claude Sonnet 5 release. The video is paired with promotions for his AI business courses and SEO tools.

videos.title: China's New AI Robot MOYA Feels Too Real (92% Human)
videos.summary: China's MOYA humanoid robot features warm skin and lifelike reactions. Boston Dynamics' Atlas is advancing toward factory autonomy, while Alibaba launched Qwen-Robot as an embodied AI system for physical machines. These developments signal humanoid robots transitioning from demonstrations to real-world deployment in warehouses, hospitals, and public spaces.

videos.title: Checkbook Diplomacy - Sarah Paine
videos.summary: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Sarah Paine on the podcast episode titled "Checkbook Diplomacy". The episode discusses diplomatic strategy and international relations approaches.

videos.title: Obsidian Just 10x’d Everyone’s Hermes Agent
videos.summary: David Ondrej demonstrates how to connect Hermes Agent to Obsidian, claiming this integration provides a 10x improvement in capabilities. The video covers practical setup for running AI agents with Hostinger.

videos.title: Delegate and schedule tasks in Claude Cowork
videos.summary: Claude Cowork is a feature that enables users to delegate work and schedule recurring tasks, including meeting preparation and periodic content scans. The tool is presented as a capability within Claude's platform.

videos.title: 3‑2‑1 Backup Rule Explained: Protect Your Data from Disaster
videos.summary: The 3-2-1 backup rule is a data resiliency strategy that specifies maintaining three copies of data across two different media types with one copy stored offsite. This approach protects against data failures and ransomware attacks through geographic and media diversity.

videos.title: Gemma Coder (Fable Distilled): WHAT.. Fable LIKE OUTPUT from a 12B MODEL?
videos.summary: Gemma 4 12B Agentic Fable 5 Compose is a fine-tuned local model optimized for coding, tool use, and agentic workflows. It claims 3.5x improvement over base Gemma 4 12B on benchmarks and runs via Ollama, LM Studio, and llama.cpp with Q4 K M quantization recommended.

videos.title: TypeScript is not TypeScript anymore...
videos.summary: TypeScript 7 is a full rewrite of the TypeScript compiler from TypeScript into Go, achieving approximately 10x faster performance. The compiler maintains API compatibility while introducing shared memory parallelism, new checkers and builders flags, and a rewritten watch mode.

videos.title: 6 Things to Know about AIE World's Fair 2026
videos.summary: The AI Engineer channel announces the AIE World's Fair 2026 conference in San Francisco, highlighting expanded content, an expo floor, research-industry collaboration, leadership tracks, vertical-focused sessions, and side events. Attendees can claim sponsor offers and access a YouTube-exclusive discount code.

videos.title: New robot waifus, GLM 5.2 craze, AI spas, new world models, new science agents: AI NEWS
videos.summary: An AI news video covering recent developments including DreamX World for world models, GLM 5.2 language models, LTX2 Trainer for video generation, robot systems including AGIbot A3 and humanoid robots, and Midjourney Medical for medical imaging applications. The episode surveys a broad range of AI tools and techniques across vision, language, and robotics domains.

videos.title: This Is The First Real Shape Of AGI: Fusion Agents
videos.summary: The video argues that multi-agent AI systems like Fusion Agents and Apps in AI agents represent the first concrete manifestation of AGI capabilities. It contrasts reasoning-layer advances (like Fable) with a new working layer that enables AI to build tools, coordinate agents, and deploy real services end-to-end.

videos.title: They loved Trajan so much they rewrote the afterlife for him - Ada Palmer
videos.summary: Ada Palmer discusses historical attitudes toward the Roman Emperor Trajan, noting that his cultural significance was so profound that religious and mythological frameworks were adapted to accommodate him in the afterlife.

videos.title: This OpenSource Repo will 10X Your Hermes Agent
videos.summary: Agent Reach is an open source repo that augments Hermes agents with web research capabilities across YouTube, LinkedIn, Reddit, X, and GitHub, plus data enrichment and contact verification via Clay integration. The system uses intelligent routing and multi-source research to reduce token usage while enabling automated lead discovery.

videos.title: GLM 5.2 is my new favorite model...
videos.summary: GLM-5.2 is an MIT-licensed open-weights model by Z.ai that ranks first on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index with a score of 51. It outperforms GPT-5.5 on the GDPval benchmark and leads Design Arena's web design leaderboard, making it the first model to surpass Claude on that benchmark.

videos.title: GLM 5.2 SUPER MODE: Here's HOW TO USE GLM-5.2 to it's FULL POTENTIAL
videos.summary: GLM 5.2 is a major upgrade over GLM 5.1 that performs well for coding tasks and can generate full applications from single prompts. When paired with Verdent, it can build complete dashboards with persistent data, responsive design, and deployment capabilities in a single workflow.

videos.title: This Claude Code Plugin Writes 94% Less Code (ponytail)
videos.summary: Ponytail is a Claude Code plugin that enforces the YAGNI principle to reduce token usage and code generation. It prioritizes native platform features and standard library solutions over custom code, achieving up to 77% cost savings and 94% less generated code according to benchmarks.

videos.title: Hermes Agent is good but, it has one problem...
videos.summary: David Ondrej reviews Hermes Agent as a capable tool but highlights a specific limitation or problem. The video appears to be promotional content featuring Apify integration.

videos.title: Shocking New AI Just Hit 12 Million Tokens With 1000x Less Compute
videos.summary: Subquadratic announced SubQ, an AI model supporting 12 million token context windows with substantially reduced inference compute via sparse attention mechanisms. The model targets enterprise document reasoning tasks including codebases, contracts, and financial filings, potentially reducing reliance on RAG and chunking pipelines.

videos.title: This Open Source Repo Solves Claude Code's Biggest Problem
videos.summary: Chase AI covers Ponytail, an open source repository that addresses limitations in Claude Code by providing architecture, memory, and observability components. The setup is presented as a three-step process to build an agentic OS around Claude Code.

videos.title: The strangest riot in papal history - Ada Palmer
videos.summary: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Ada Palmer on an unusual riot in papal history. This is a historical discussion that does not directly concern AI industry developments.

videos.title: Different Profiles, Different Personalities—A Game Changer
videos.summary: Keith AI presents content about how different profiles and personalities serve as a meaningful innovation. The video is titled "Different Profiles, Different Personalities, A Game Changer" suggesting system personalization or behavioral differentiation is impactful for AI applications.

videos.title: Beyond chatbots: How AI agents built a full OS, web apps, and more
videos.summary: Google demonstrated AI agents executing complex multi-day engineering pipelines to build non-trivial software including a managed desktop OS and optimized 3D web applications. The video showcases the Agent Development Kit for enabling long-running autonomous agents.

videos.title: Best AI Logo Generator: How to Make Logo With AI (2026 Comparison)
videos.summary: A hands-on comparison of five AI logo generators: Design.com, BrandCrowd, Canva, Wix Logo Maker, and Vistaprint, tested on the same business brief to evaluate quality, AI editing capabilities, licensing, and integration features. The video identifies which tools deliver client-ready results and which have hidden paywalls or overstate AI features.

videos.title: The data black hole at the center of AI
videos.summary: Dwarkesh Patel argues that the enormous scale of training data is the invisible foundation ("black hole") holding together AI capabilities, and that humans underestimate how much more data these models train on compared to human lifetime experience. The essay compares human and AI sample efficiency as a key factor in understanding AI progress.

videos.title: Governed agents: Looker + MCP explained
videos.summary: Google Cloud presents Looker's MCP server integration, which bridges large language models to data through a governed semantic layer. The approach uses the Model Context Protocol and Agent Development Kit to enable agents grounded in validated business models rather than direct SQL generation.

videos.title: Why ChatGPT isn't good at building automations
videos.summary: David Ondrej argues that ChatGPT has limitations when building automations. The video promotes Creao as an alternative platform designed for automation tasks.

videos.title: My AI Memory Now Follows Me Across Every Tool!
videos.summary: Walrus Memory is a persistent memory layer that operates outside individual AI agents, allowing context and memories to transfer across different AI tools and providers. The system encrypts memories owned by the user and allows selective access control across agents like Claude, Codex, and Pi with simple setup.

videos.title: Stop Prompting Claude. Start Loop Engineering.
videos.summary: Loop Engineering (Claude Loops) is a framework that replaces traditional prompting with iterative agentic loops. The approach uses five core building blocks: trigger, execution skills, goal and verification, output and memory, and feedback mechanisms to create self-improving AI workflows.

videos.title: One Shared #Hermes From Any Device (Local UI, VPS Brain)
videos.summary: Keith AI demonstrates a distributed architecture where Hermes can be shared across multiple devices using a local UI connected to a remote VPS-hosted inference backend. This enables local interface access while centralizing model compute resources.

videos.title: Why Desktop #Hermes Beats #Telegram for Real Work
videos.summary: Keith AI argues that Desktop Hermes outperforms Telegram for productive work. The video covers sessions, profiles, and artifacts as key features distinguishing Hermes.

videos.title: Microsoft’s new AI models & bots dominate the internet
videos.summary: A Cloudflare study shows agentic AI bots generate 57.4% of web requests globally. The Mixture of Experts podcast panel debates whether this represents a fundamental shift in web interaction or a continuation of existing bot dominance patterns.

videos.title: ego lite: I gave Claude Code & Codex a browser to run web automation — here's what happened
videos.summary: ego lite is a Chromium-based browser that provides AI agents with separate workspaces to run web automation tasks without disrupting the user's normal browsing. It integrates with tools like Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor, offers semantic webpage snapshots, and supports migration of Chrome data including bookmarks, extensions, and login sessions.

videos.title: Master Hermes Desktop in 34 mins
videos.summary: A tutorial on setting up and using Hermes Desktop, a control center for managing AI agents with features including local LLM integration, VPS deployment, remote gateway access via Tailscale, profiles, sessions, sub-agents, and cron blueprints. The video demonstrates switching from Telegram-based control to a dedicated desktop application for better workflow organization.

videos.title: Microsoft And DeepSeek Are Launching New AI Together
videos.summary: Microsoft is launching Copilot Cowork as a general-availability multi-model AI agent platform with usage-based billing. DeepSeek could become a lower-cost model option on Azure. Microsoft is expanding enterprise AI capabilities by routing work across OpenAI, Claude, Cowork 1, and potentially DeepSeek models.

videos.title: Gemma 4 and the AI Edge Gallery: On-Device AI Gets an Upgrade
videos.summary: Gemma 4 is released with improved performance, with the 2B model matching last year's 27B dense model. The Google AI Edge Gallery app has exceeded five million model downloads in its first month, supporting offline inference with agent skills framework including Wikipedia, WebFetch, HTML rendering, and game integrations. MCP integration is coming to Android and iOS with persistent chat history features.

videos.title: Putin's playbook - Sarah Paine
videos.summary: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Sarah Paine on Putin's strategic approach and decision-making framework. The episode analyzes geopolitical playbook and foreign policy strategy.

videos.title: Hermes Agent Tutorial: How to Setup & Use Open Source AI Agent for Beginners
videos.summary: Hermes Agent is an open-source AI agent framework available on OpenRouter with near 200K GitHub stars. It enables local deployment on Mac or Windows with capabilities including Telegram integration, persistent memory, autonomous skill generation, and cron job scheduling for tasks like morning news briefs.

videos.title: Matt Pocock’s Agentic Engineering Workflow (just copy him)
videos.summary: David Ondrej presents Matt Pocock's agentic engineering workflow as a teachable and reproducible approach. The video demonstrates practical techniques for AI-assisted coding and engineering methodologies.

videos.title: Gemma Playground: AI Edge Gallery
videos.summary: Ian Ballantyne demonstrates Gemma running locally on a Pixel 10 Pro via the Google AI Edge Gallery app. The demo covers image understanding, audio transcription, agent skills for app routing, and multimodal inference all offline without network connectivity.

videos.title: 3 patterns to build long-running AI agents
videos.summary: Video from Google Cloud Tech explains three core design patterns for production-grade long-running AI agents: event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation. These patterns enable agents to manage multi-day workflows beyond stateless chatbot capabilities.

videos.title: Artifacts in Claude Code: share your work as it happens
videos.summary: Claude Artifacts is a beta feature available on Team and Enterprise plans that transforms raw AI-generated output such as data, mockups, and JSON into visually rendered pages shareable with team members. The feature enables users to generate and display artifacts directly within Claude Code sessions.

videos.title: Antigravity SDK: Building a digital simulated world
videos.summary: Google Antigravity SDK enables creation of multi-agent social simulations with virtual avatars that interact autonomously in simulated environments. The SDK was demonstrated at Google I/O with a space station simulation where attendee avatars interacted in real time.

videos.title: Enterprise-managed auth for MCP connectors
videos.summary: Anthropic has released enterprise-managed authentication for MCP (Model Context Protocol) connectors, allowing administrators to authorize and authenticate connectors organization-wide through identity providers without requiring individual employee setup. The feature is designed to streamline deployment and security for enterprise organizations.

videos.title: 3 takeaways on Software Ecology™️
videos.summary: Google for Developers presents three takeaways on Software Ecology, examining how easy AI code generation affects the future of software systems. Speaker Adam Bender discusses this topic as part of Google AI product commentary.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code explains Andrej Karpathy's Bigram Language Model in a 10-minute tutorial. The video covers tokenization, embedding, training, loss functions, backpropagation, and the AdamW optimizer as foundational components for understanding larger language models.

videos.title: 7 tips for using Antigravity 2.0 on enterprise codebases, coding phase
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech presents seven practical tactics for managing Antigravity 2.0, an AI coding agent, on enterprise codebases. The guidance covers encoding team standards into agent skills, committing implementation plans to Git, automated test-and-fix loops, UI validation via headless browser, async task offloading, architecture visualization, and pre-review automated checks.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Is Missing Half the Internet… Until Now (Agent-Reach)
videos.summary: Agent-Reach is an open source tool that enables AI agents to access real internet context across Western and Chinese platforms including Twitter/X, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, and Xiaohongshu. It addresses limitations of coding agents that break when needing external research, social feedback, tutorials, and non-English information from the internet.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: Loop Engineer is a framework for building AI agents with setup instructions and practical templates. The video provides a GitHub repository for implementation and an AI builder workshop for learning the methodology.

videos.title: How to build an AI Agent and MCP Server (step-by-step)
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech tutorial demonstrates how to build AI agents using Model Context Protocol (MCP), an open standard that enables agents to connect with live data and real-world tools. The video shows a practical example of integrating Google Trends with a blog writing agent using Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK).

videos.title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
videos.summary: Sandipan Bhaumik from Databricks presents the Production AI Playbook, a framework for deploying agents at enterprise scale. The framework rests on five pillars: evaluation with numerical success metrics defined upfront, observability with comprehensive agent tracing for regulatory compliance, data foundation rigor, multi-agent orchestration patterns, and governance systems that caught 47 PII breaches in testing before launch.

videos.title: Build a Real-World Multi-Agent AI App with MCP + LangGraph | Part 2
videos.summary: Tutorial on building a multi-agent travel planning system that integrates LangGraph agents with Model Context Protocol servers for real-time flight and weather data. The system includes PostgreSQL-backed memory, Tavily search, and Streamlit UI deployment.

videos.title: The New Kimi K2.7 Model is VERY Impressive
videos.summary: Cole Medin reviews Kimi K2.7, an open-source coding-focused agentic model from Moonshot AI. He praises its capabilities relative to its size and cost.

videos.title: Building AI Agents for Real-World Problems & Workflows
videos.summary: IBM Technology explains how AI agents operate in production systems with workflows, policies, and orchestration. The video discusses agent coordination, task enforcement, rule application, and alignment with human decision-making in real-world deployments.

videos.title: FULLY FREE GLM-5.2 + Z-Code: This is ACTUALLY GOOD!
videos.summary: Z Code is a new Codex-like coding agent from Z AI built specifically for GLM models. It offers a free tier with 5 million tokens per day for GLM-5.2, includes features like project generation and DevTools integration, and is positioned as an alternative to OpenAI Codex.

videos.title: 6 INSANE Claude Cowork Use Cases to Save Hours (AI Workflow Automation)
videos.summary: A YouTube tutorial showcasing six Claude Cowork workflows for AI-driven automation: morning briefs, voice-consistent content generation, inbox triage, research report generation, live dashboards, and mobile agent control. The video demonstrates practical applications of Claude Cowork beyond basic chatbot usage.

videos.title: When you realize that you’re the senior dev now.
videos.summary: A YouTube video from Google for Developers featuring Pete King reflecting on the moment someone realizes they have become a senior developer. The piece treats the transition to seniority as a moment of unexpected responsibility.

videos.title: How to build a custom vision agent
videos.summary: A tutorial on building a custom vision agent that combines live webcam input with generative capabilities. The agent uses Google Gemini, Model Context Protocol, and Veo 3 to process images and natural language prompts including real-time ASL conversations.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: Top AI engineers use automated loops to prompt agents repeatedly rather than manually prompting them. Cole Medin built a TypeScript dashboard that runs Pi in a loop with database persistence and orchestrates parallel agent workers, addressing reliability and cost concerns in agentic workflows.

videos.title: China’s New AI Is 6X More Efficient Than Claude
videos.summary: Moonshot AI released Kimi K2.7 Code, an open-weight coding model designed for coding agents working through real codebases. The model achieves competitive efficiency compared to closed alternatives like Claude.

videos.title: How a Metal Box Changed the World - Sarah Paine
videos.summary: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Sarah Paine on how the standardized shipping container transformed global commerce and society. The discussion explores technological disruption and systemic change driven by infrastructure innovation rather than AI.

videos.title: From Idea to Code: Building with Miro and Kiro — Live
videos.summary: A live session demonstrating how Miro and Kiro work together to move from idea to working code. Seán Winters and Brian Beach show visual collaboration with AI-assisted development through prototyping and building, with examples from hackathons in Paris, Amsterdam, LA, and New York.

videos.title: Every Level of Hermes Agent Explained
videos.summary: A tutorial series explaining seven levels of Hermes agent complexity, from basic prompting to fully autonomous systems that orchestrate multiple LLMs and execute scheduled tasks. Covers integration with Claude, ChatGPT, DeepSeek, and Grok through a unified framework.

videos.title: Converting SQL Server types and rules to PostgreSQL
videos.summary: Google Cloud shows how Gemini, an AI assistant, can help developers convert SQL Server database schemas and code to PostgreSQL equivalents. The tool explains conversion concepts like mapping user-defined types to PostgreSQL DOMAINs, enabling developers to learn PostgreSQL best practices during migration.

videos.title: Microsoft's launched OpenClaw?
videos.summary: Microsoft Scout is an AI agent integrated into Microsoft's ecosystem (Teams, email, calendar) that proactively prepares work materials and takes actions before users request them, functioning like an autonomous chief of staff. The tool learns from plain language descriptions of work and anticipates needs across Microsoft's productivity suite.

videos.title: Coding with ADK to beat a text adventure game
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech livestream featuring speakers Tilde Thurium and Abdel Sghiouar demonstrating the Agent Development Kit (ADK) for coding an AI agent to play a text adventure game.

videos.title: AI Fact or Fiction: The Fable Ban, Tokenmaxxing, Saaspocolypse — With Ara Kharazian
videos.summary: Ara Kharazian, lead economist at Ramp, discusses which AI adoption narratives are real versus exaggerated. Topics include actual spending by businesses, whether Anthropic has overtaken OpenAI in adoption, the "tokenmaxxing" phenomenon, and whether the predicted SaaS apocalypse from AI is materializing.

videos.title: I Tried the Open Source ElevenLabs Alternative (Voicebox)
videos.summary: Voicebox is a free open-source local AI voice studio offering voice cloning, text-to-speech, system-wide dictation, agent voice output, and MCP integration. The video compares Voicebox to cloud alternatives like ElevenLabs, highlighting its local-first and privacy-focused approach for unlimited generation without cloud dependencies.

videos.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: Agents often claim to search the web but may actually hit blocks, CAPTCHAs, or stale data while hallucinating success. The session demonstrates real web access via Bright Data's Web MCP to address anti-bot systems, JavaScript rendering, and CAPTCHA handling for reliable agent citations and real-time results.

videos.title: How to Start a Business Using AI: From Idea to Launch in One Day
videos.summary: A video review evaluates Rocket 1.0 as a solution for maintaining context across AI workflows in business development. The reviewer tests the tool's ability to replace manual integration between research, note-taking, code generation, and tracking systems, and provides an honest assessment of its strengths and weaknesses.

videos.title: AI agents can manage your passwords. Should we let them? Plus: The biggest Patch Tuesday ever.
videos.summary: A podcast episode discussing whether AI agents should manage passwords and detect compromised credentials, examining cybersecurity hygiene practices. videos.title: Minimax Code + M3 : Crazy Alternative to Claude,Codex!

videos.title: POV: A new feature enters the chat. 🕺✨

The content is presented as a point-of-view skit with an emphasis on relatable engineering experiences.

The model is integrated with Claude and available through Z.ai platform and online chat.

videos.title: Fable 5 Replacement Just Dropped: Fusion (Fable Level AI)

A budget Fusion configuration achieved performance close to Fable 5 on deep research benchmarks at roughly half the cost.

videos.title: The Real Reason Giordano Bruno Was Burned at the Stake - Ada Palmer
videos.summary: Ada Palmer discusses the historical circumstances surrounding Giordano Bruno's execution by the Inquisition. The title suggests a reexamination of the conventional narrative about Bruno's burning at the stake.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes

videos.title: The Briefing: Financial Services
videos.summary: Major banks are deploying Claude across their organizations. Anthropic hosted a virtual briefing session where leaders shared their implementations in financial services.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 with Harness Engineering & Loops Will Do Everything.
